DISCOVERY 612, RULING AFTER HEARING: DEFENDANT TH AGRICULTURE & NUTRITION, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL DOLORES POLILLO'S DEPOSITION APPEARANCE AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR HER RESUMED DEPOSITION ON A DATE NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 12, 2008. THE DATE IS TO BE AGREED UPON AFTER MEET AND CONFER AND IS TO BE NOTICED BY BERRY AND BERRY. THE DEPOSITION IS NOT COMPLETE AND IS NOT STRICKEN. A TRANSCRIPT OF THE TESTIMONY GIVEN IN THE ABSENCE OF THE MOVING DEFENDANTS SHALL BE PREPARED AND PROVIDED TO DEFENDANTS. DEFENDANTS SHALL RECEIVE THE TRANSCRIPT AT LEAST 10 DAYS PRIOR TO THE DEPOSITION. WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO ANY TESTIMONY SHALL BE MADE AT LEAST THREE DAYS PRIOR TO THE RESUMPTION OF THE DEPOSITION. THE ISSUANCE OF SANCTIONS TO BE DETERMINED. ALL JOINDERS ARE GRANTED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. COURT REPORTER: QUYEN DO, CSR # 12447

MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF EXPERT WITNESS
Off Calendar Nov-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jamil White, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Dolores Polillo, et al
Walter Rundin, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Golden Gate Drywall

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED:Plaintiff Dolores Polillo's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Michael Medeiros And Production Of Documents is granted. Defendant has failed to show that Mr. Medeiros in fact would be entitled to a protective order. Liberty Mutual identifies certain factors to be considered by the court in determining whether an apex deposition should be allowed. Defendant's opposition makes none of those evidentiary showings.
Defendant's general objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Objections must be made individually to individual requests. Any objections made based upon privilege shall be made at the deposition and documents withheld pursuant to any claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity. Objections of burden based upon the In Re: Complex Litigation deposition are overruled. Meet and confer is required to narrow the breadth of the requests.
Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. CSR: Mary Jackson, Advantage Court Reporters, Tel# (8180 409-9803)

Off Calendar Nov-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Michael David Smith, Esq., for defendant and moving party: TH Agriculture & Nutrition, Inc.; Jessica J. Han, Esq., appeared via telephone for Defendant and opposing party: General Electric Co.; Jesse Boyd, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Kelly MooreKurt Putnam, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Bondex

HEARING HELD ON Defendant TH Agriculture & Nutrition, LLC's Motion To Compel Dolores Polillo's Deposition Appearance And For Protective Order; And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
RULING AFTER HEARING: Defendant TH Agriculture & Nutrition, LLC's Motion To Compel Dolores Polillo's Deposition Appearance And For Protective Order is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for her resumed deposition on a date no later than December 12, 2008. The date is to be agreed upon after meet and confer and is to be noticed by Berry and Berry. The deposition is not complete and is not stricken. A transcript of the testimony given in the absence of the moving defendants shall be prepared and provided to defendants. Defendants shall receive the transcript at least 10 days prior to the deposition. Written objections to any testimony shall be made at least three days prior to the resumption of the deposition. The issuance of sanctions to be determined. All joinders are granted. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. Court Reporter: Quyen Do, CSR # 12447

Ruling:
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall's Motion To Compel Further Responses is granted. The motion was timely filed. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. Plaintiff's objections were a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $900, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
